seo.description: Savor authentic Hakata-style tonkotsu ramen at Ramen Taro in Higashiyamato, Tokyo. 48-hour slow-cooked broth, handmade noodles, and fresh ingredients since 1985.

seo.aboutDescription: Discover the heritage of Ramen Taro — from a humble Fukuoka street stall in 1985 to an authentic ramen destination in Tokyo. Meet our team and culinary philosophy.
seo.contactDescription: Contact Ramen Taro for reservations and directions. Located in Higashiyamato, Tokyo. Open daily for lunch and dinner. Call +81 3-8765-4321.

about.title: About Ramen Taro | Our Story Since 1985
about.content: Founded in 1985, Ramen Taro began as a humble street stall in the vibrant Fukuoka district. Over the decades, we have evolved into a cornerstone of authentic Japanese dining in Tokyo, dedicated to preserving the time-honored traditions of ramen making while selectively embracing modern culinary innovations. Our journey from a small stall to a multi-award winning restaurant is a testament to our commitment to quality, authenticity, and the pursuit of the perfect bowl.

founder.name: Takeshi Yamamoto
founder.bio: With over 30 years of experience in traditional Japanese ramen making, Takeshi Yamamoto founded Ramen Taro as a humble Fukuoka street stall in 1985. His dedication to the craft of slow-cooked tonkotsu broth has earned the restaurant a loyal following across Tokyo. Chef Yamamoto personally oversees the selection of ingredients and the preparation of our signature 48-hour broth every single day.

additionalContent: Our secret lies in our signature 48-hour tonkotsu broth, which is simmered slowly using premium pork bones to achieve a rich, creamy, and deeply savory texture that has become our trademark. This painstaking process ensures that every spoonful is packed with umami and nutritional value. Every morning, our skilled chefs handcraft fresh alkaline noodles with the perfect 'bite' and texture to complement our various soup bases.

Beyond the food, Ramen Taro is a deep celebration of Japanese culture and hospitality. We strive to create an atmosphere of 'Omotenashi'—wholehearted hospitality where the needs of the guest are anticipated and met with grace. Whether you're a long-time regular or a first-time visitor from abroad, we want your experience to be more than just a meal; we want it to be an unforgettable journey into the heart of Japanese culinary arts.

We believe that a great bowl of ramen has the power to bring people together. That's why we maintain an open kitchen concept, allowing our guests to witness the passion and precision that goes into every bowl. From the precise temperature of the water to the exact thickness of the chashu pork, no detail is too small for our attention.

team.name: Takeshi Yamamoto
team.bio: With over 30 years of experience, Takeshi is the heart of our kitchen.

team.bio: Hanako ensures every guest feels at home with her warm hospitality.

team.bio: Kenji's passion for fresh ingredients brings innovation to our menu.
